tasks.title: Responsive collapsible sidebar with mobile drawer
tasks.description:
The AppLayout sidebar is currently a fixed `w-56` (224px) `<aside>` that's always visible and has no responsive behavior — zero `md:`, `sm:`, or `lg:` classes exist anywhere. Add responsive sidebar collapse with mobile drawer.

**Implementation:**

1. **Desktop (md+ breakpoints):** Keep sidebar visible by default. Add a collapse toggle button (hamburger/chevron icon) in the sidebar header that toggles between `w-56` and `w-14` (icon-only rail). When collapsed: hide nav text labels, show only icons, hide the user email/role text, keep avatar visible. Use `transition-all duration-200` for smooth animation. Persist preference in `localStorage` under key `task-toad-sidebar-collapsed`.

2. **Mobile (<md breakpoint):** Hide sidebar by default (`hidden md:flex` on the aside). Add a hamburger button in a new thin top bar (`md:hidden`) that opens the sidebar as an overlay drawer. The drawer should slide in from the left with a backdrop overlay (`bg-black/40`). Clicking the backdrop or any nav link closes the drawer. The top bar should show the TaskToad logo + hamburger button.

3. **Notification overlays:** The NotificationCenter (line 226) and NotificationSettings (lines 228-242) use `absolute` positioning relative to the sidebar. Ensure they still work when sidebar is collapsed (desktop) or in drawer mode (mobile). They may need to be repositioned or rendered in a portal.

4. **Quick search button:** The `⌘K` quick search button (line 147-157) should remain visible in both collapsed and expanded states — show just the search icon when collapsed.

5. **Card migration in Skeleton.tsx:** While here, replace the inline card pattern in KanbanColumnSkeleton (line 45: `bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-lg p-3 shadow-sm border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700`) with `<Card padding="sm">`. Import Card from `./shared/Card`.

Do NOT add responsive behavior to ProjectToolbar or ProjectDetail — those are separate concerns for a future wave. Only touch AppLayout and Skeleton.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Desktop (md+): sidebar toggles between expanded (w-56) and collapsed (w-14) rail with smooth animation. Preference persists in localStorage. Mobile (<md): sidebar hidden by default, hamburger button opens drawer overlay, clicking backdrop or nav link closes it. Notification overlays work in both states. Skeleton.tsx uses Card component. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build passes.

tasks.title: Badge adoption round 2: taskdetail components (6 files)
tasks.description: Continue Badge component adoption from Wave 24. The Badge component (`shared/Badge.tsx`) currently has 5 variants: success (green), warning (amber), danger (red), info (blue), neutral (slate). Several taskdetail components use inline pill patterns that should be migrated. **Badge.tsx enhancement needed first:** The current 5 variants don't cover purple (used for epics, merged PRs, code gen) or orange (used for high priority). Add two new variants to Badge: - `purple`: `'bg-purple-100 text-purple-700 dark:bg-purple-900/30 dark:text-purple-400'` - `accent`: `'bg-orange-100 text-orange-700 dark:bg-orange-900/30 dark:text-orange-400'` Update the `BadgeVariant` type union accordingly. **Migrations:** 1. **TaskAIReviewSection.tsx** (lines 37-49): Replace the inline `Approved` and `Changes Requested` with `` and ``. These badges contain SVG icons as children — Badge's `inline-flex items-center` already handles icon+text layout. 2. **TaskAIHistory.tsx** (lines 24-31): Replace `FEATURE_BADGES` color record. Map: Instructions -> info, Code Gen -> purple, Expand -> success, Plan -> accent, Review -> danger, Bug Report -> warning. Line 114 cached badge: `cached`. 3. **TaskTitleEditor.tsx** (lines 57-62): Replace task type ternary. Map: epic -> purple, story -> info, subtask -> neutral, default -> neutral. 4. **TaskGitHubSection.tsx** (lines 69-74): Replace PR state ternary. Map: MERGED -> purple, OPEN -> success, CLOSED -> danger. 5. **TaskDetailPanel.tsx** (lines 22-29): The `TYPE_COLORS` record maps custom field types to colors. Simplify: map DROPDOWN -> info, TEXT -> neutral, NUMBER -> accent, DATE -> warning, CHECKBOX -> success, then any remaining -> neutral.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Badge.tsx has 7 variants (added purple + accent). All 5 taskdetail files import and use Badge. No inline bg-*-100 text-*-700 patterns remain in these files (except where Badge className override is used). pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes. Visual: badges render with correct colors matching the originals.

tasks.title: Badge adoption round 2: modals and panels (5 files)
tasks.description: Continue Badge adoption for modal and panel components. This task depends on task-002 completing the Badge variant expansion (purple + accent) but these files don't overlap. **Migrations:** 1. **CodePreviewModal.tsx:** Line 295: `Style guide active` -> `Style guide active`. Line 476-480: This is a toggle *button* styled like a badge — leave as-is (Badge is not a button). 2. **MeetingNotesDialog.tsx** (lines 25-30): Replace `priorityStyles` record. Map: critical -> danger, high -> accent, medium -> info, low -> neutral. Update usage sites to use ``. 3. **ProjectHealthPanel.tsx** (lines 25-35): Replace `statusColors` and `severityColors` records. Map health: healthy -> success, at-risk -> warning, critical -> danger. Map severity: high -> danger, medium -> warning, low -> neutral. Note: emerald-100/emerald-700 is close enough to Badge's success (green-100/green-700). 4. **TaskPlanApprovalDialog.tsx** (lines 6-11): Replace `PRIORITY_STYLES` record same as MeetingNotesDialog. Lines 90-96: priority badge and hours badge — use `` and `~{formatHours(...)}`. Line 106-108: dependency badge with border — use ``. 5. **WebhookSettings.tsx** (lines 40-45): Replace `STATUS_BADGE` record. Map: success -> success, retrying -> warning, failed -> danger, pending -> neutral. Update the `` at the usage site to ``. For all files: import Badge from the correct relative path (`./shared/Badge` for components).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: All 5 files import and use Badge. No inline bg-*-100 text-*-700 pill patterns remain (except CodePreviewModal diff toggle button which stays as a button). pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes.

tasks.title: Add ARIA accessibility to ProjectToolbar template and export overlays
tasks.description:
The DropdownMenu component already has full ARIA compliance (role="menu", role="menuitem", aria-haspopup, aria-expanded, keyboard nav). However, the template and export overlays in ProjectToolbar.tsx are plain `<div>` elements with no ARIA attributes or keyboard management. These can't use DropdownMenu since they contain custom content (select, input, buttons), but they need basic ARIA and keyboard support.

**Template overlay (lines 286-331):**
1. Add `role="dialog"` and `aria-label="Create from template"` to the overlay div
2. Add focus management: when the overlay opens, auto-focus the select element (or first focusable element). When it closes (Escape or Close button), return focus to the overflow menu trigger. Use a ref + `requestAnimationFrame` pattern.
3. Escape key already closes the overlay (existing useEffect at lines 58-76). Verify it works and that focus returns to trigger.

**Export overlay (lines 334-369):**
1. Add `role="menu"` and `aria-label="Export options"` to the overlay div
2. Add `role="menuitem"` to each export button
3. Add keyboard navigation: track `activeIndex` state, ArrowDown/ArrowUp cycle through items, Enter activates. Use an `onKeyDown` handler on the menu div.
4. Add focus management: auto-focus first item on open, return focus to trigger on close. Use refs for the menu items.

**Both overlays:**
- The overflow DropdownMenu triggers these via onClick handlers in items array. After DropdownMenu closes (it auto-closes on item click), the overlay opens. Focus should move to the overlay.
- Ensure `tabIndex={-1}` on container divs if they need to receive focus programmatically.
- The overlays already close each other when one opens (line 277-278: `setShowExportMenu(false)` / `setShowTemplateMenu(false)`).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Template overlay has role="dialog" + aria-label + auto-focus on open + focus return on close. Export overlay has role="menu" + role="menuitem" + arrow key navigation + auto-focus + focus return. Both accessible via keyboard. pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint passes.

tasks.title: PWA docs: cache invalidation strategy + navigateFallback verification
tasks.description:
Clean up two documentation follow-ups open since Waves 20-21.

**1. PWA cache invalidation strategy (Wave 20 follow-up):**
Document in `.claude-knowledge/decisions.md` how to force a service worker update on breaking API changes. Read `apps/web/vite.config.ts` to understand the current PWA config (workbox settings, runtime caching, navigateFallback). Then add a section to `decisions.md` covering:
- How Vite PWA autoUpdate works (checks for new SW on page load, auto-activates)
- How to force cache bust: bump the SW version or change precache manifest (any code change triggers new SW)
- For breaking API changes: the NetworkFirst strategy for `/api/*` means API responses aren't stale-cached
- Emergency SW unregister: `navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations().then(r => r.forEach(r => r.unregister()))` in browser console

**2. PWA navigateFallback behavior (Wave 21 follow-up):**
Verify in `apps/web/vite.config.ts` that `offline.html` is only served for navigation requests. Check the `navigateFallbackDenylist` — it should cover `/api/` but also any other non-navigation paths (e.g., source maps, manifest). If the denylist is incomplete, add entries for `/assets/`, `/sw.js`, `/workbox-*.js`. Document findings in decisions.md.

This is primarily documentation with a possible minor config fix to vite.config.ts.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: decisions.md has a PWA section covering: cache invalidation strategy, SW update lifecycle, navigateFallback denylist verification. Any needed denylist entries added to vite.config.ts. pnpm build passes (vite config is valid).
